summary: This is a casual weekend newsletter where Joe Reis shares personal updates on using AI to automate tasks, announces upcoming community events in Atlanta, Phoenix, and London, and curates interesting reads from the week. The post also provides an update on his upcoming data modeling book, targeting late January 2026 publication.
key_points: Experimenting with Claude to automate Apple Reminders tasks
AI orchestration of multi-app workflows is promising but still unreliable
Data modeling book manuscript nearly complete, Book 1 targeting January 2026
Promoting Coalesce community events and MotherDuck London meetup
money_quotes: In general, the ability to chain multiple apps together and have AI orchestrate tasks and workflows is pretty damn cool.
It's not always perfect (the AI's time out a lot and don't finish tasks), but I expect the models and capabilities will improve a ton.
I love clanker art, especially when it creates three hands
key_insight: AI-powered task orchestration across multiple applications is becoming practical for personal productivity, though reliability issues remain a work in progress.
